messages.content: match the word with the definition, remembering the words at the start and end of a list A. intentional learning B. george sperling C. (818) 662-4254 D. primacy-recency effect E. episodic memory F. semantic memory G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water

A. intentional learning - the process of actively and consciously acquiring new knowledge or skills
B. George Sperling - an American cognitive psychologist known for his research on visual perception and iconic memory
C. (818) 662-4254 - a phone number unrelated to the definitions provided
D. primacy-recency effect - the tendency to better remember the first and last items in a list
E. episodic memory - the ability to recall specific personal experiences, events, or episodes
F. semantic memory - the capacity to remember general knowledge, facts, and concepts
G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water - unrelated to the definitions provided

What word matching "remembering the words at the start and end of a list"A. intentional learning B. george sperling C. (818) 662-4254 D. primacy-recency effect E. episodic memory F. semantic memory G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water
The word that matches "remembering the words at the start and end of a list" is D. primacy-recency effect.

What word matches "an example of chunking" A. intentional learning B. george sperling C. (818) 662-4254 D. primacy-recency effect E. episodic memory F. semantic memory G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water
The word that matches "an example of chunking" is A. intentional learning.

what word matches "memorizing foreign language words or phases" A. intentional learning B. george sperling C. (818) 662-4254 D. primacy-recency effect E. episodic memory F. semantic memory G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water
The word that matches "memorizing foreign language words or phrases" is A. intentional learning.

what word matches "tested people's sensory memory" A. intentional learning B. george sperling C. (818) 662-4254 D. primacy-recency effect E. episodic memory F. semantic memory G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water
The word that matches "tested people's sensory memory" is B. George Sperling. George Sperling conducted research on visual perception and iconic memory, which involves the sensory memory system.

what word matches "context dependence" A. intentional learning B. george sperling C. (818) 662-4254 D. primacy-recency effect E. episodic memory F. semantic memory G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water

The word that matches "context dependence" is G. scuba divers who remember words above or below water. Context dependence refers to the fact that memory retrieval can be influenced by the context in which the information was encoded or learned. The example given alludes to the idea that scuba divers may recall words differently depending on whether they were learned above or below water.
true or false, retriveing information is the same as intentional learning

False. Retrieving information is the process of recalling or accessing previously learned information from memory. Intentional learning, on the other hand, refers to the deliberate and conscious effort to acquire new knowledge or skills. While retrieval is part of the learning process, they are not the same thing.

what can you learn from the scuba divers who remembered certain words better either above or below water? A. long-term memory is more likely to help the test subjects instantly recall information B. people remember information best when they're in the original place where they learned it C. memories with strong emotional attachments are easier to recall than other memories D. people often attach their surrounding to their memories

The answer would be D. people often attach their surroundings to their memories. The example of scuba divers remembering certain words better depending on whether they were learned above or below water suggests that the environmental context in which information is learned can play a role in memory retrieval. This highlights the concept of context-dependent memory, where memories are often linked to the specific context or surroundings in which they were formed.
wang stares into the sky and notices the clouds are fluffy white. what is this an example of? A. surface processing B. deep processing C. incidental learning D. information retrieval

This is an example of surface processing. Surface processing refers to the superficial or shallow processing of information, focusing on its physical or sensory features. In this case, Wang is simply observing the fluffy white clouds without engaging in deeper cognitive processing or attempting to encode the information in a meaningful way.

what is a similarity between incidental and intentional learning? A. both allow knowledge to be forgotten to make room in your brain B. both resemble deep processing C. both bring knowledge into your brain D. btoh require adequate sleep

A similarity between incidental and intentional learning is that both bring knowledge into your brain (Option C). In both cases, learning takes place, and new information is acquired and stored in memory. However, it is important to note that the key distinction lies in the intentionality aspect - intentional learning involves a conscious effort and active engagement to acquire knowledge, while incidental learning occurs without explicit intention or awareness.

select multiple answers, what does the prefrontal cortex help you to do? A. determine small from large B. work toward a goal C. delay gratification D. understand future consequences E. fortell the future F. control urges

The prefrontal cortex helps you to:
B. work toward a goal.
C. delay gratification.
D. understand future consequences.
F. control urges.

how do semantic and episodic memories differ? A. one remembers general ideas, and the other remembers specific events B. each is only a wordy description that doesn't really exist C. one helps your memory to be accurate, and the other allows for fantasy thoughts D. each is processed in a different part of the brain

The correct answer is A. Semantic memory remembers general ideas, concepts, and knowledge, while episodic memory remembers specific events, experiences, and personal episodes. They differ in the type of information they store, with semantic memory focusing on general knowledge and episodic memory focusing on specific personal experiences.
overlearning is _ A. a method for improving your memory B. immediately available for recall C. an elaborate process that involves practice, memorization, recitiation and rehearsal D. all of the above

The correct answer is D. all of the above. Overlearning refers to the process of continued practice or repetition beyond the point of initial mastery. It is considered a method for improving memory as it strengthens the memory trace and enhances long-term retention. Overlearned information becomes immediately available for recall and is more resistant to forgetting. The process of overlearning often involves elaboration, practice, memorization, recitation, and rehearsal.

strokes and alzheimers disease are serious conditions because A. both conditions are inherited B. both conditions often result in severe memory loss C. both conditions kill brain cells and cause instant death D. all of the above

The correct answer is B. both conditions often result in severe memory loss. Strokes and Alzheimer's disease are serious conditions due to the cognitive impairment and memory loss they can cause. However, it is not accurate to say that both conditions are inherited (A) or that they cause instant death by killing brain cells (C). While there can be a genetic component to Alzheimer's disease, it is not solely inherited, and strokes are not primarily inherited conditions. Additionally, while strokes and Alzheimer's disease can have serious consequences, they do not typically cause instant death.

H.M.'s operation on the hippocampus showed that A. long-term memory is severely affected B. all but A C. damage to that brain area results in amnesia D. seizures can be cured, but memory will be affected E. short-term memory doesn't work well either

The correct answer is B. all but A. H.M.'s operation on the hippocampus did not demonstrate severe impairment in long-term memory (A). It did, however, show that damage to that brain area (the hippocampus) results in amnesia (C). The operation performed on H.M. was intended to alleviate his severe seizures, and while it was successful in reducing the seizures, it also resulted in anterograde amnesia, meaning he could not form new long-term memories from that point forward. It did not directly address seizures being cured or short-term memory impairments (D and E).

what did you notice about diagnosing a mental illness? A. getting a diagnosis for a mental disorder is sometimes complex B. getting a diganosis for a mental disorder is relatively easy C. getting a diagnosis for a mental disorder does not require a trained professional D. getting a misdiagnosis for a mental disorder never happens

The correct answer is A. getting a diagnosis for a mental disorder is sometimes complex. Diagnosing a mental illness can indeed be a complex process. It typically involves a comprehensive evaluation by a trained mental health professional who assesses various factors such as symptoms, medical history, and psychosocial factors. Mental health diagnoses often require careful consideration and can involve ruling out other potential causes of the symptoms. It should be noted that while efforts are made to ensure accurate diagnoses, misdiagnosis (option D) can occur, making the process more challenging. Option C is incorrect because a trained professional is typically required for an accurate diagnosis.

how are physical illness and mental disorders alike? A. both physical illness and mental disorders have all the same symptoms B. both physical and mental disorders have none of the same symptoms C. both physical illness and mental disorders have only one similar symptom D. both physical illness and mental disorders can sometimes have the same symptom

The correct answer is D. both physical illness and mental disorders can sometimes have the same symptom. While physical illnesses primarily affect the body and mental disorders primarily affect the mind, there can be overlap in symptoms. For example, a person experiencing chronic pain could have a physical illness such as fibromyalgia or a mental disorder such as depression or somatic symptom disorder. Similarly, symptoms such as fatigue, sleep disturbances, or appetite changes can be seen in both physical illnesses and mental disorders. It is important to note that symptoms alone are not sufficient to determine a diagnosis; a comprehensive evaluation by healthcare professionals is required to differentiate between physical and mental health conditions.

how can you recognize a mental disorder? A. by getting a diagnosis from a teacher B. by getting a diagnosis from a friend C. by getting a diagnosis from a parent D. by getting a diagnosis from a psychologists

The correct answer is D. by getting a diagnosis from a psychologist. Recognizing a mental disorder involves seeking professional evaluation from a qualified mental health professional, such as a psychologist. Mental health professionals assess various factors, including symptoms, history, and functioning, to make an informed diagnosis. While friends, teachers, or parents can provide support and observations, a formal diagnosis can only be given by a trained and licensed professional.
which word matches "disturbance of normal behavioral and emotional functioning" A. catharsis B. mental disorder C. treatment

The word that matches "disturbance of normal behavioral and emotional functioning" is B. mental disorder. A mental disorder is a condition that significantly affects a person's thoughts, emotions, behaviors, and overall functioning in a way that deviates from what is considered typical or healthy.
which word matches "releasing emotions that have been held within" A. catharsis B. treatment

The word that matches "releasing emotions that have been held within" is A. catharsis. Catharsis refers to the process of expressing and releasing strong emotions, typically through some form of therapeutic or artistic means, in order to achieve emotional relief or purification.

what could you do if you wanted to seek help but could not afford it? select multiple answers A. talk to a trusted adult B. visit a counselor at school C. deal with it on your own D. call a free helpline E. tell only your best friend

If you wanted to seek help but could not afford it, the following options are available to you:
A. Talk to a trusted adult - They may be able to provide guidance, support, and help you explore affordable or free resources.
B. Visit a counselor at school - Many schools have counselors who can provide support and resources, even if you are unable to afford outside help.
D. Call a free helpline - Many helplines offer free and confidential support for various mental health concerns. They can provide guidance and resources.
E. Tell only your best friend - While it can be helpful to confide in a close friend for emotional support, it is important to also seek professional help or utilize other available resources for comprehensive support. Relying solely on a friend may not be sufficient for addressing mental health concerns.

what is the importance of having access to therapy? A. a person with a mental illness does not have a chance of improving with therapy B. a person with a mental disorder can improve with therapy C. a person with a mental illness does not need access to therapy D. people who suffer from a mental disorder do not have any options to get therapy

The importance of having access to therapy is primarily highlighted by option B. A person with a mental disorder can improve with therapy. Therapy can provide individuals with a safe and supportive environment to address their mental health concerns, develop coping strategies, gain insight, and work towards improving their overall well-being. Therapy offers evidence-based treatments tailored to specific mental health conditions and can be effective in managing symptoms, improving functioning, and enhancing quality of life. It is crucial to emphasize that therapy is not the only option, but it does play a significant role in the treatment and management of mental health conditions.
explain in a few sentences how income has historically affected access to therapy

Historically, income has played a significant role in determining access to therapy. Higher-income individuals have had greater access to therapy due to the ability to afford private healthcare plans, which often cover mental health services. On the other hand, lower-income individuals have faced barriers in accessing therapy, including lack of insurance coverage, limited availability of affordable mental health services, and the cost of out-of-pocket expenses. This income disparity has contributed to inequities in mental healthcare access and treatment outcomes. Efforts have been made to reduce these disparities through initiatives such as community mental health programs and expanded insurance coverage for mental health services.

every thursday, kimberly meets with a therapist and other people about her challenges with depression. what is the format of the therapy session? A. cognitive therapy B. group therapy C. one-on-one therapy D. couples therapy

The format of the therapy session described is B. group therapy. In group therapy, individuals with similar challenges or mental health concerns come together to discuss and explore their experiences under the guidance of a therapist. This format allows participants to gain support, learn from others, share insights, and develop coping strategies in a collaborative and structured setting.
when a individual meets with a therapist alone at scheduled times it is called A. one-on-one therapy B. couples therapy C. group therapy D. cognitive therapy

The correct answer is A. one-on-one therapy. When an individual meets with a therapist alone at scheduled times, it is referred to as one-on-one therapy or individual therapy. In this type of therapy, the focus is on the specific needs, concerns, and goals of the individual, and the therapy sessions are conducted in a private setting between the client and the therapist.
describe the different formats for therapy? in only acouple sentences

Therapy can be conducted in various formats. One-on-one therapy, also known as individual therapy, involves private sessions between a therapist and a client focusing on the individual's needs. Group therapy involves a therapist leading a session with a small group of individuals facing similar challenges, encouraging sharing and support. Couples therapy, or relationship therapy, involves a therapist working with a couple to address issues and improve their relationship dynamics. Finally, cognitive therapy is a specific approach that focuses on identifying and changing negative or unhelpful thought patterns and beliefs. These different formats offer unique benefits and cater to the specific needs and preferences of individuals seeking therapy.
